statutes.title: Certain activities relating to material involving sexual exploitation of minors
elements: Knowing transportation, receipt, distribution, or possession
Visual depiction of minor engaged in sexually explicit conduct
Interstate or foreign commerce, or computer
statutes.investigation_notes: Hash matching (PhotoDNA) commonly used for possession/distribution; P2P network monitoring standard

statutes.title: Definitions — child pornography
statutes.investigation_notes: Definition section — critical for charging; covers AI-generated CSAM post-PROTECT Act

statutes.title: Reporting requirements of electronic service providers (CyberTipline)
elements: ESP must report apparent CSAM to NCMEC CyberTipline
Must preserve evidence 90 days pending law enforcement request
Location data and IP address required in report
statutes.investigation_notes: CyberTipline reports from NCMEC are primary investigative lead; must be corroborated before warrant application

statutes.title: Coercion and enticement
elements: Individual under 18 to engage in sexual activity
statutes.investigation_notes: Online undercover operations — agent posing as minor; jurisdiction established by interstate communication

statutes.title: Transportation of minors
elements: Transportation of minor in interstate/foreign commerce
With intent minor engage in sexual activity
statutes.investigation_notes: Travel element key; sting operations — defendant need not actually travel to complete offense

methodology.description: CSAM investigation methodology standards — training signal for ABBY

phases.legal_notes: CyberTip alone not sufficient for probable cause; must independently corroborate IP-to-subscriber link

sources: ISP subscriber records (18 U.S.C. § 2703(c) — administrative subpoena)
documentation: Return of subpoena with subscriber info
Chain from CyberTip IP to physical address
phases.legal_notes: Subscriber info = § 2703(c) subpoena; content requires warrant; Carpenter applies to historical location

sources: Affidavit incorporating CyberTip + ISP return + corroboration
documentation: Description of items to be seized (devices, storage media)
phases.legal_notes: Must establish nexus between residence/device and CSAM; collectors tend to retain material (staleness less of an issue); particularity required for digital devices — see Ybarra v. Illinois

sources: All digital devices, storage media, gaming consoles, smart devices
documentation: Chain of custody initiated at scene
phases.legal_notes: Plain view doctrine applies to observed CSAM during lawful search; scope of warrant governs what may be seized

methodology: Hash compare known CSAM databases (NCMEC, IWF, Project VIC)
phases.legal_notes: Hash matching to known CSAM databases provides probable cause and corroboration; examiner must be prepared to testify to methodology

documentation: Submission of unidentified victim images to NCMEC
phases.legal_notes: Victim identification is separate investigative obligation; examiners should submit unknown victims even if charging is complete

tools: Project VIC International VICS hash database
hash_matching.evidentiary_value: Hash match to known CSAM database is strong corroborating evidence; not conclusive — must view matched file to confirm
hash_matching.court_acceptance: Widely accepted; examiner must explain hash matching in plain terms; defense may challenge false positive rate
hash_matching.daubert_notes: PhotoDNA has published error rate; methodology peer reviewed; generally accepted in forensic community
